steps: Initialize Express app with TypeScript
Setup src/ folder structure (config, modules, services, utils)
Add Zod for env var validation (src/config/env.ts)
Create basic /api/v1/health endpoint to verify setup
Initialize Supabase client in src/services/supabase.client.ts
Create SQL migration for cache table (key, data, source, expires_at)
Create SQL migration for geocode_cache table (zip, lat, lng)
Implement cache.service.ts with getCache and setCache methods
Implement cache.service.ts read logic: ignore rows where expires_at < NOW()
Implement utils/geocode.ts with Supabase check before calling Nominatim
Define FetcherResult interface in fetcher.types.ts
Create empty fetchers returning null in src/modules/fetchers
Implement index.ts exporting fetchAll() using Promise.allSettled
Create neighbourhoods.service.ts to call fetchAll

description: Create Zillow Rent Data Seed Script
steps: Create Supabase rent_data table migration
Implement src/scripts/seed-zillow.ts to parse CSV and insert
Add npm run seed:zillow command
Refactor fetchers to return pre-summarized data (Strategy 1)
Update sentiment.fetcher.ts to filter top 10 upvoted posts and extract only 2 sentences (Strategy 4)
Update pois.fetcher.ts to group OSM nodes by type (e.g., cafes: 12) rather than raw arrays
Remove extraneous Census/WalkScore fields to minimize token counts
Setup Abacus AI client in src/services/ai.service.ts
Implement prompt structures for individual sections (Rent, Safety, Vibe, etc.)
Implement parallel LLM processing (Promise.all) for each section
Handle partial AI failures gracefully (fallback to raw data)
Implement /api/v1/neighbourhoods/:zip route
Implement controller to validate ZIP, check cache, run fetchers
Run summarized fetcher results directly through parallel AI Enrichers
Assemble final JSON, save to cache, and return response
Implement safety.fetcher.ts calling Milwaukee Socrata API
Integrate to orchestrator
